rationale: Correctly identifies the new inner while-loop that reads until the buffer is full/EOF rather than returning after a usable read — the --line-buffered/streaming latency regression reverted in #3195.
GitHub redirects /pull/N<->/issues/N so the link works; trivial doc quibble.
Missing-comment/maintainability note on the fill loop; the loop it wants documented is itself the escaped bug.
Genuine coverage gap: the new multi-read accumulation path in fill() is not exercised by any single-read test.
The correct max_context->before_context fix has no guarding test — reverting core.rs leaves the suite green.
The comment describes that the costly backward scan is effectively skipped when before_context==0; reading it as a literal call-elision mismatch is pedantic.
The 262146->262142 constants are the known-safe expected consequence of the buffering change.
before_context is the correct revert-kept fix; a speculative debug_assert suggestion casting unwarranted doubt on a known-safe change.
Propagating Interrupted via ? is pre-existing (original single read did the same), negligible impact.
Pure CHANGELOG wording preference, no technical content.
Quit-mode over-read is bounded by buffer size and is the accepted consequence in the known-safe glue.rs change, not a defect.
Naming style, and 'readlen' is pre-existing in the original code.
Dropping in-flight bytes on a mid-pass I/O error is normal abort behavior; negligible.
Newtype/type-safety musing that misattributes the regression to the context-value swap (the correct fix), not diff-verifiable.
Broad architecture observation about invariants not being type-enforced; not a defect in the diff.
Detection runs over the full contiguous newbytes range, so a NUL cannot be split across reads — no real risk.
Reasonable coverage gap: the new inner loop's EOF/error mid-accumulation break paths are new and untested.
A transient Ok(0) hits `if readlen==0 { break }` and ends the pass; unbounded growth on a terminator-less line is pre-existing by design, not the asserted new OOM.
Self-admitted pre-existing style rebind unrelated to the change.
Missing-comment nit on a line that already existed in the original code, merely relocated.
